title_en: The LEGO Speed Champions Time Machine From Back to the Future Has Plummeted to Its Lowest Price Yet at Amazon
summary_en: The LEGO Speed Champions Time Machine from Back to The Future has hit a new low price at Amazon of $22.39. This is 20% off its full price of $27.99.
body_en:
Amazon's Prime Day sale event may not be until next week, but we're already spotting some excellent early deals at the retailer worth picking up right now. For LEGO fans, the LEGO Speed Champions set of the DeLorean from Back to The Future is one that's popped up on our radar that's hit a new all-time low price at the retailer of $22.39 ( see it here ).
Usually it's listed for $27.99, so this is a nice opportunity to save a few extra dollars on it. If you're a Back to The Future fan, or shopping for someone in your life who is, it definitely looks like quite a fun build to work on, too.
Compared to its much bigger counterpart for adults, this LEGO DeLorean set is angled towards younger builders, around the age of nine and older, and comes with 357 pieces. These create the DeLorean from Back to the Future, complete with fun additions like the lightning rod. It can even be rebuilt into its flying version with wheels that angle sideways and Mr. Fusion energy reactor.
The details don't stop there, though, as you'll also find rear air vents, a cockpit, the flux capacitor, and a time calculator in this build. Of course, you'll also get two minifigures of Doc Brown and Marty McFly that you can place in or around the car once it's all pieced together.
Before Prime Day even begins, this is quite a nice offer to make a move on for this LEGO DeLorean set at Amazon. If you've had your eye on it, now is definitely the time to add it to your cart.
If you're looking for even more exciting LEGO deals like this one, you'll definitely want to make sure your calendar is marked for June 23 for Amazon Prime Day . We'll be keeping an eye out for even more discounts as they drop at the retailer, both in the lead-up to the sale event and during its four-day run.
Hannah Hoolihan is a freelancer who writes with the guides and commerce teams here at IGN.
